# BeachCheck

> Free, independent tool showing real-time water quality, water temperature, waves and wind for Toronto's supervised swimming beaches — built for swimmers, paddlers and SUP riders who want one glance instead of five tabs.

BeachCheck covers all of Toronto's supervised beaches: Marie Curtis Park East, Sunnyside, Hanlan's Point, Gibraltar Point, Centre Island, Ward's Island, Cherry, Woodbine, Kew-Balmy, Bluffer's Park and Rouge.

A beach reads "swim" when its latest E. coli sample is under the City of Toronto's posting limit (100 / 100 mL), "no swim" at or above it, and "no data" outside the June–September sampling season. This is not official safety advice; always check for posted lifeguard flags at the beach itself.

## Data sources

- Water quality & posted swim status: City of Toronto open data (https://open.toronto.ca/dataset/toronto-beaches-water-quality/)
- Water temperature: Open Water Data buoys (https://www.openwaterdata.com/)
- Wave height: NOAA/ECCC buoy 45159, Lake Ontario (https://www.ndbc.noaa.gov/station_page.php?station=45159)
- Wind & air temperature: Open-Meteo (https://open-meteo.com/)

## Links

- Site: https://beachcheck.ca/
- Source code: https://github.com/anderskravis/beachcheck
